title: Other Player
html:
Now that the crying has stopped and she is properly upright, Lucas gets a clearer look at her: thin almond-shaped eyes, heart-shaped lips, about his age, average-looking in the way that most people are average-looking. He is not going to say this out loud. The world ending does not change the basic calculus of what you say to someone you just met.

"So. What are you doing out here?" he asks.

She looks up at him and then back at the road and then, apparently deciding that talking is better than standing in silence next to a stranger who just stabbed four zombies, tells him.

She had been at a karaoke venue the night of the 20th, a big one in the city center, with a group of girls from school. The next day was Sunday, no class, so the plan was to stay until dawn. At midnight the sound went off in everyone's heads — the same broadcast Lucas heard at his table over birthday cake — and no one in the venue thought it was anything unusual. A glitch in the audio system, maybe. Then one of the girls screamed and fell down, and when the girl next to her reached over to help, the first girl bit her. The helper kept shouting for her to stop. She didn't stop.

It went through the room fast. Several people were on the floor and biting before the rest understood what was happening. The whole venue was a single corridor of private rooms, a hundred doors open onto the same hallway, and when the screaming started rolling out of each room in sequence it sounded like something moving down the building. She and a few others got out through the main entrance and ran.

Outside was worse. Car crashes happening simultaneously on multiple streets, the kind that follow from drivers going suddenly limp at the wheel. She kept running. A few of the girls caught up with her on a side street and one of them had the idea to get to the outskirts and wait for it to settle. They found a taxi. Halfway to the outskirts the driver turned, reached back over his seat, and grabbed the girl sitting directly behind him.

"I was behind the left door, so I just opened it and got out," she says, which Lucas notes is a practical decision made under pressure by someone who claims she had barely killed a fly before this week.

She made it to the outskirts and found a construction site with no workers — Saturday midnight, so the site was empty — and stayed in it for three days on the food still in her system from the party. Today hunger finally won over caution and she came out to find a convenience store, which is when the zombie jumped her at the entrance, and her scream pulled two more off the street.

"Until you appeared," she finishes, looking at the road.

Lucas considers his own version of January 21st: birthday cake at midnight, went to sleep, woke up to a boat missing and some fish with unusual blood. He clears his throat. "Anyway. What's your name?"

She blinks at the subject change. "I'm Lucy. Lucy Lyons." She looks at him with the remnant of the cry still around her eyes. "Nice to meet you."

"Lucas," he says. "Hi." He tilts his head slightly. "What level are you?"

"Level?" She looks at him the way someone looks at a question asked in the wrong language.

"System level. From the system." He can see this is not landing. "You haven't opened your system." It is not a question.

"I rarely play games," she says.

"It's not a game." He says it without judgment because there is no useful purpose served by judgment here. "Opening System." His holographic screen appears in front of him and he turns slightly so she can see the readout.

---

Name: Lucas Tharla

Age: 16 years old

Race: Human

Gender: Male

Class: None

Subclass: None

Subclass: None

Subclass: None

Level: 5 (210/400)

Stats:

Agi: 14 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Str: 24 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Int: 25 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Vit: 25 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Unallocated Stats Point: 2

Skills:

Unallocated Skills Point: 5

Coins: 15

Achievements Completed: 2 ᐃ

Items: 3 ᐃ

---

Lucy looks at the display, looks at the wall of the alley behind it, and looks back at Lucas. 'Is he damaged in the head.' She says nothing, which is its own kind of answer.

"You can't see it," he says. "Right." He closes the screen. "Come with me." He takes her by the wrist and starts walking.

She goes with him because there is no immediately obvious alternative, but she is thinking things. He can tell she is thinking things by the way her pace is slightly off and she keeps tilting her head down. He doesn't ask. Whatever she is imagining is going to be resolved in about three minutes regardless.

They reach the white building. He leads her in.

The moment the door closes behind her, Lucy stops walking. She looks at the white tile, the white walls, the empty room, and the black table in the center. She looks at Lucas. Something in her expression has gone very careful and closed in the way that people's expressions go when they have decided to be very calm about something they are not calm about at all. Her legs appear to not be working at their full capacity.

"Touch that," Lucas says, pointing at the button on the table.

Lucy's expression does something complicated. She looks at his finger, then at the table, then at the button. She walks to the table with the posture of someone who has committed to a decision and is not reviewing it anymore. She puts her finger on the button and closes her eyes. Presses it.

Nothing happens immediately. She opens her eyes.

"Why would you—" she starts.

The sound goes off in her head and a holographic display appears above the table, text running down it.

---

[System Class Change Detected, Commencing System Profile!]

[Opening System!]

---

---

Name: Lucy Lyons

Age: 16 years old

Race: Human

Gender: Female

Class: None

Subclass: None

Subclass: None

Subclass: None

Level: 0 (0/10)

Stats:

Agi: 7 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Str: 12 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Int: 17 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Vit: 32 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Unallocated Stats Point: 0

Skills:

Unallocated Skills Point: 0

---

She reads it. She reads it again. Then she looks at Lucas, who points at the black table. She turns back. Above the pedestal a second display has appeared, next to her profile.

---

[Class Change Initiated!]

[Error, The Player Haven't Reach the Level Requirement Yet!]

---

Lucy stares at the error message. Her mouth is open slightly. She is processing at what appears to be maximum capacity and is not quite getting there.

Then a third notification appears.

---

Achievement Clear:

[Bronze] Eighty-Eight to Discover the Class Change Building.

Rewards:

Basic Katana [Uncommon]

---

A black katana materializes on the tile floor beside the pedestal: about 60 centimeters long, plain grip, no ornamentation. Lucy picks it up because it appears in front of her and picking up things that appear in front of you is a reasonable response when your brain has run out of other responses.

"A katana," she says. Her voice has gone flat in the way that voices go flat when there is nothing left to modulate with.

Lucas nods. He is watching her the way you watch someone who is either about to be fine or about to sit down on the floor.

"Is that it?" she asks.

---

[System Items Detected, Updating System Profile!]

---

---

Name: Lucy Lyons

Age: 16 years old

Race: Human

Gender: Female

Class: None

Subclass: None

Subclass: None

Subclass: None

Level: 0 (0/10)

Stats:

Agi: 7 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Str: 12 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Int: 17 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Vit: 32 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Unallocated Stats Point: 0

Skills:

Unallocated Skills Point: 0

Coins:

Achievements Completed: 1 ᐃ

Items: 1 ᐃ

---

Lucy looks at the updated screen. She looks at the katana in her hand. She looks at the white walls of the room, which offer nothing useful. She has been saved from zombies, cried on a stranger's hand, been led into a white room by that stranger and told to press a button, received a holographic profile, gotten an error message, been awarded a sword from the air, and is now looking at a second version of her own holographic profile.

"I need a moment," she says.

"Take a short one," Lucas says. He checks the door. "We should eat something and work out where we're going. You haven't had food in how long?"

"Since the party," she says. "That was four days ago."

He opens the pouch and pulls out a tin of canned meat and a bag of sunflower seeds and sets them on the pedestal beside the empty holographic interface. "Eat," he says, and then he sits down on the floor with his back against the wall and opens his own system to check the numbers while she processes whatever she needs to process.
